tools.name: bash
tools.description: Execute a shell command and return the output
example_queries: list files in the current directory
show git status
find all python files larger than 100KB
count lines of code in src/
check what's using port 8080
run the test suite
install numpy with pip
show disk usage

tools.name: file_read
tools.description: Read the contents of a file at the given path
example_queries: read README.md
show me the config file
what's in pyproject.toml
read the first 20 lines of main.py
show me the test file for auth

tools.name: file_write
tools.description: Write content to a file, creating it if it doesn't exist
example_queries: create a hello.py that prints hello world
write a .gitignore for python
save these notes to output.txt
create a Dockerfile for this project

tools.name: file_edit
tools.description: Search and replace a unique string in a file
example_queries: change the port from 8080 to 3000 in config.py
fix the typo in the README
replace the old API URL with the new one
rename the function from old_name to new_name

tools.name: glob
tools.description: Find files matching a glob pattern
example_queries: find all python files
what test files exist
show me all markdown files
find config files in the project

tools.name: grep
tools.description: Search for a regex pattern in files
example_queries: find where the database URL is defined
search for TODO comments
find all imports of numpy
where is the login function called

tools.name: python_exec
tools.description: Execute Python code and return output
example_queries: calculate 2^100
parse this JSON and extract the names
generate 10 random passwords
what's the fibonacci of 50

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch a web page URL and return text content
example_queries: fetch the contents of that URL
what does the API docs page say
read the GitHub README for that repo

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search the web and return results
example_queries: search for python asyncio tutorial
find the latest MLX release notes
what's the current version of numpy

categories.single_tool: One tool call answering one question
categories.multi_sequential: Tool A result feeds into Tool B
categories.multi_parallel: Multiple independent tools in one response
